title: Evidence Of A Real UFO Crash Collected By CIA
summary: This interview discusses evidence of a potential UFO crash near Hartford, Connecticut, involving unusual metal debris that appeared to be losing mass. The material, containing aluminum and barium, connects to research conducted at Bonson Labs and relates to Townsend Brown's work.

key_claims.claim: A greenish fireball crashed near Hartford, Connecticut, setting a shed on fire and leaving metal debris
The cover story was that it was a Russian satellite that exploded, but this was debunked
Townsend Brown went to the crash location and took the material before Project Blue Book could retrieve it
Analysis of the material showed it appeared to be losing mass, and objects nearby also lost mass
The material contained aluminum and some form of barium
Barium appeared both on a chalkboard at Bonson Labs where Townsend Brown worked and in his Winter Haven proposal
